description: A fried or baked sweet dough pastry, often ring-shaped or filled, and commonly finished with sugar, glaze, or icing. Styles vary widely by country and bakery tradition.
core_ingredients: Fat such as butter or oil
preparation_steps: Prepare a dough or batter, depending on the style.
Shape the donuts or portion the batter.
Fry until golden or bake, depending on the recipe.
Cool slightly, then coat with sugar, glaze, or other toppings.

health_advice.benefits: Can be enjoyable as an occasional treat and provides quick energy from carbohydrates.
health_advice.risks: Often high in refined flour, sugar, and fat, especially when fried and heavily topped.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Treat as an occasional dessert rather than an everyday breakfast item.

dietary_notes.note: Vegetarian versions are common; vegan and gluten-free versions require recipe changes.

allergens: Possible soy depending on glaze or frying ingredients
